Step 24a
⍞ Materials: Fifty Steps in Sight Singing

➜ INTRO
We're continuing to learn how to sing in multiple keys this term. But before you sing, warm up your voice.
∞ Audio Link: Warm-Ups
∞ Video Link: Warm-Ups

➜ LISTEN & PRACTICE
Write down the sol-fa syllables and note names for the exercise at the very top of p.27 to familiarize yourself with the key of F. Then sing with the recording. (See Appendix for note names.)
∞ Audio Link: Step 24a Answer Key

Mystery Tune

➜ RECAP
Review concepts from the last lesson.

➜ WARM UP
Prepare your voice for singing.
∞ Audio Link: Warm-Ups
∞ Video Link: Warm-Ups

➜ PRACTICE
Write out the sol-fa syllables and the names of the top notes of this melody in the key of F. (Hint: The song starts on the So below Do.) Then try to sing it yourself. Do you recognize the tune? Check your work at the very end with the Appendix and by playing the tune via the link given.
∞ Audio Link: Mystery Tune Sheet Music & Audio

Singing at Sight

➜ RECAP
Review concepts from the last lesson.

➜ WARM UP
Prepare your voice for singing.
∞ Audio Link: Warm-Ups
∞ Video Link: Warm-Ups

➜ PRACTICE
Flip through a hymnal and pick a song in the key of C or F. See if you can figure out the rhythm and the sol-fa (it's a puzzle). (Hint: Remember hymns often end on do.) Then use a piano or the online pitch pipe to hear your first note and sing!
∞ Audio Link: Online Pitch Pipe

➜ RECAP
Review concepts from the last lesson.

➜ WARM UP
Prepare your voice for singing.
∞ Audio Link: Warm-Ups
